title: Avoid Excessive Return Statements in Methods
description: Methods having too many return statements increases the essential complexity as the flow of execution is disrupted every time a return statement is encountered. This makes it harder to comprehend the method's logic. This rule is aimed to limit the use of return statements in a method.
recommendation: Keep the number of return statements to a minimum within a method. If possible, restructure the method's logic to avoid breaking the flow frequently.
impact: Improves code readability and maintainability by reducing the method's complexity.

checks.message: Keep the number of return statements to a minimum within a method. If possible, restructure the method's logic to avoid breaking the flow frequently.
